matches.x: Be glad in the LORD, and rejoice, ye righteous: and shout for joy, all [ye that are] upright in heart.
Rejoice, O my heart, and cry unto the Lord, and say: O Lord, I will praise thee forever; yea, my soul will rejoice in thee, my God, and the rock of my salvation.
Therefore, let us glory, yea, we will glory in the Lord; yea, we will rejoice, for our joy is full; yea, we will praise our God forever. Behold, who can glory too much in the Lord? Yea, who can say…
Rejoice in the LORD, ye righteous; and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.

Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the LORD.

For the righteous LORD loveth righteousness; his countenance doth behold the upright.
For the LORD loveth judgment, and forsaketh not his saints; they are preserved for ever: but the seed of the wicked shall be cut off.
The king's strength also loveth judgment; thou dost establish equity, thou executest judgment and righteousness in Jacob.
In this the children of God are manifest, and the children of the devil: whosoever doeth not righteousness is not of God, neither he that loveth not his brother.
He doth execute the judgment of the fatherless and widow, and loveth the stranger, in giving him food and raiment.
But let him that glorieth glory in this, that he understandeth and knoweth me, that I [am] the LORD which exercise lovingkindness, judgment, and righteousness, in the earth: for in these [things] I…

And one cried unto another, and said: Holy, holy, holy, is the Lord of Hosts; the whole earth is full of his glory.

The Gemara asks: What are these ten utterances? Presumably, they are the utterances introduced by the words “and God said” in the story of Creation in the first chapter of Genesis. However, there are only nine of these utterances and not ten. The Gemara answers: The expression: “In the beginning” (Genesis 1:1) is also considered an utterance, as it is written: “By the word of the Lord were the…

Thus saith the Lord GOD unto these bones; Behold, I will cause breath to enter into you, and ye shall live:
And I will lay sinews upon you, and will bring up flesh upon you, and cover you with skin, and put breath in you, and ye shall live; and ye shall know that I [am] the LORD.
And I will gather my people together as a man gathereth his sheaves into the floor.

And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together.

The depth saith, It [is] not in me: and the sea saith, [It is] not with me.

Come against her from the utmost border, open her storehouses: cast her up as heaps, and destroy her utterly: let nothing of her be left.

And the anger of the Lord is kindled, and his sword is bathed in heaven, and it shall fall upon the inhabitants of the earth.
Fear before him, all the earth: the world also shall be stable, that it be not moved.

And the Lord commanded him that he should arise. And he arose and stood before him.

Also, I give unto you a commandment that ye shall continue in prayer and fasting from this time forth.

And the land of Judah shall be a terror unto Egypt, every one that maketh mention thereof shall be afraid in himself, because of the counsel of the LORD of hosts, which he hath determined against it.

But ye have set at nought all my counsel, and would none of my reproof:

TO the chief Musician, to Jeduthun, A Psalm of David. Truly my soul waiteth upon God: from him [cometh] my salvation.
PSALM XXXIII. God’s goodness, power, and providence. EJOICE in the Lord, 0 ye righteous; to praise the Lord. is comely for the upright in- heart.
Happy [art] thou, O Israel: who [is] like unto thee, O people saved by the LORD, the shield of thy help, and who [is] the sword of thy excellency! and thine enemies shall be found liars unto thee;…
O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD: he [is] their help and their shield.
Ye that fear the LORD, trust in the LORD: he [is] their help and their shield.
O Israel, trust thou in the LORD: he [is] their help and their shield.
Unless the LORD [had been] my help, my soul had almost dwelt in silence.
And Asa cried unto the LORD his God, and said, LORD, [it is] nothing with thee to help, whether with many, or with them that have no power: help us, O LORD our God; for we rest on thee, and in thy…
Happy [art] thou, O Israel: who [is] like unto thee, O people saved by the LORD, the shield of thy help, and who [is] the sword of thy excellency! and thine enemies shall be found liars unto thee;…
O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD: he [is] their help and their shield.
Ye that fear the LORD, trust in the LORD: he [is] their help and their shield.
O Israel, trust thou in the LORD: he [is] their help and their shield.
The LORD [is] my strength and my shield; my heart trusted in him, and I am helped: therefore my heart greatly rejoiceth; and with my song will I praise him.

PSALM XXXIII. God’s goodness, power, and providence. EJOICE in the Lord, 0 ye righteous; to praise the Lord. is comely for the upright in- heart.
3 And the works of truth and the works of deceit are written upon the hearts of men, and each one of them the Lord knoweth.
Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the LORD.
And when I desire to rejoice, my heart groaneth because of my sins; nevertheless, I know in whom I have trusted.
Behold, my beloved brethren, remember the words of your God; pray unto him continually by day, and give thanks unto his holy name by night. Let your hearts rejoice.
Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the LORD.
But let all those that put their trust in thee rejoice: let them ever shout for joy, because thou defendest them: let them also that love thy name be joyful in thee.
And after this manner was the language of my father in the praising of his God; for his soul did rejoice, and his whole heart was filled, because of the things which he had seen, yea, which the Lord…
And when I desire to rejoice, my heart groaneth because of my sins; nevertheless, I know in whom I have trusted.
And again, verily I say unto you, my servant George Miller is without guile; he may be trusted because of the integrity of his heart; and for the love which he has to my testimony I, the Lord, love…
But I have trusted in thy mercy; my heart shall rejoice in thy salvation.
But let all those that put their trust in thee rejoice: let them ever shout for joy, because thou defendest them: let them also that love thy name be joyful in thee.
For after this manner in the old time the holy women also, who trusted in God, adorned themselves, being in subjection unto their own husbands:
In whom ye also [trusted], after that ye heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation: in whom also after that ye believed, ye were sealed with that holy Spirit of promise,
Blessed [be] the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which according to his abundant mercy hath begotten us again unto a lively hope by the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
And after I had traveled for the space of many hours in darkness, I began to pray unto the Lord that he would have mercy on me, according to the multitude of his tender mercies.
And then Alma cried unto the Lord, saying: O Lord our God, have mercy on this man, and heal him according to his faith which is in Christ.
And he began to cry unto the Lord, saying: O Lord, have mercy; according to thy abundant mercy which thou hast had upon the people of Nephi, have upon me, and my people.
Having gone according to their own carnal wills and desires; having never called upon the Lord while the arms of mercy were extended towards them; for the arms of mercy were extended towards them,…
Help me, O LORD my God: O save me according to thy mercy:
3 And the works of truth and the works of deceit are written upon the hearts of men, and each one of them the Lord knoweth.
Blessed [be] the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which according to his abundant mercy hath begotten us again unto a lively hope by the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
While many thousands of others truly mourn for the loss of their kindred, yet they rejoice and exult in the hope, and even know, according to the promises of the Lord, that they are raised to dwell…
Let Israel hope in the LORD: for with the LORD [there is] mercy, and with him [is] plenteous redemption.
The LORD taketh pleasure in them that fear him, in those that hope in his mercy.
Behold, the eye of the LORD [is] upon them that fear him, upon them that hope in his mercy;
But sanctify the Lord God in your hearts: and [be] ready always to [give] an answer to every man that asketh you a reason of the hope that is in you with meekness and fear:
